spec.goal: Author a Symphony-aligned fallback policy spec for Maestro-owned lanes that makes manual-intervention and automatic-recovery boundaries explicit without coupling the model to the current local skill catalog.
success_criteria: A normative spec artifact defines the decision matrix for continue, wait, retry, retained-lane resume, manual intervention, and ready-to-land outcomes.
The spec distinguishes stable runtime concepts from the current local workflow implementation and cites Symphony as the sole external design reference.
Local spec navigation is updated so future work on XY-173, XY-174, and XY-175 can implement against the accepted policy.
The policy clarifies that review-request and bounded resend behavior are orchestration concerns implemented by local workflow adapters rather than core domain states.
constraints: Use openai/symphony README and SPEC as the only external design standard.
Treat AGENTS.md and installed skills as local execution-context inputs, not the core domain model.
Keep the work scoped to documentation and issue-record alignment in this lane.

tasks.title: Capture the abstract fallback model
tasks.objective: Synthesize the current Maestro runtime boundaries and the latest Symphony architecture boundaries into a stable set of runtime decisions, authoritative signals, and action classes for owned lanes.
inputs: AGENTS.md and current workflow skills as local execution context
outputs: A concrete outline for the fallback policy spec
A confirmed list of stable runtime decisions and human-intervention boundaries
verification: The outline separates stable runtime concepts from local workflow implementation details.
The outline covers both pre-review failures and post-In-Review outcomes.

tasks.title: Write the fallback policy spec
tasks.objective: Create the in-repo normative spec document and any necessary spec-index links so the fallback policy becomes an authoritative reference for later implementation issues.
verification: The new spec includes the required routing header.
The new spec contains a concise decision table and explicit recovery boundaries.
The spec index links or references the new policy topic.

tasks.title: Validate and sync progress
tasks.objective: Validate the saved plan and the edited docs, then record the resulting artifact back to the authority issue so follow-on implementation issues can rely on it.
outputs: Issue progress note linking the drafted artifact

triggers: Symphony alignment requires changing the spec structure or success criteria.
The fallback policy needs additional lifecycle scope that overlaps XY-160 materially.
Local workflow constraints force a different artifact shape than the current plan assumes.

evidence: 2026-03-19: Confirmed root checkout has a local WORKFLOW.md modification, so implementation work for XY-176 moved to clone-backed lane .workspaces/XY-176.

2026-03-19: Aligned the design reference to openai/symphony README.md and SPEC.md while keeping AGENTS.md and installed skills as local execution-context inputs rather than the core runtime model.

2026-03-19: Added docs/spec/system_maestro_owned_lane_policy.md with the stable action classes, decision table, human-intervention boundaries, and automatic-recovery prerequisites for Maestro-owned lanes.
2026-03-19: Updated docs/spec/index.md to route future readers to the owned-lane fallback policy spec.
2026-03-19: Verification passed for plan validation, cargo make fmt-check, and git diff --check in .workspaces/XY-176.
2026-03-19: Reopened the spec drafting task to clarify that review-request submission and bounded resend logic are orchestration-owned behaviors rather than core domain states.

2026-03-19: Added orchestration-owned convergence-budget policy for pre-PR self-review and post-review repair churn, routing exhausted budgets to manual_intervention_required instead of endless patch loops.
2026-03-19: Re-ran cargo make fmt-check and git diff --check after the orchestration-budget refinement.
